TUTORIAL_START: Before your adventure begins, let's go over the basic controls. Use the arrow keys, WASD, or right-click to move, and press Enter to open the chat window and send messages.
TUTORIAL_TRAINING_GUIDE: You will not be able to leave the city until your character reaches level 6. Head to the Training Ground first to learn the basics. Training Ground Location: Militard 122:55, 123:55 / Pagohol 22:59, 22:60.
TUTORIAL_TRAINING_ENTER: You've entered the Training Ground! Let's start with an introduction to Guards and Guard Zones.
TUTORIAL_GUARD_ZONE: Guard zones are safe areas. Use the markers to distinguish safe and combat areas.
UI_RUNTIME_PK_ZONE_NONE: Safe zone. PK is not allowed.
UI_RUNTIME_PK_ZONE_FACTION: Faction PK zone. You can attack players from other nations only.
UI_RUNTIME_PK_ZONE_FREE: Free PK zone. You can attack any player.
UI_RUNTIME_PK_ZONE_AVAILABLE: PK-enabled area. (except when PK status is [PK OFF])
TUTORIAL_INVENTORY_OPEN: Open your inventory and check your starter items.
TUTORIAL_ITEM_CHECK: Check the equipment and items you received. Equipment can be worn before use.
TUTORIAL_EQUIP_CHECK: Equip your starter gear. Equipment loses durability and will need repairs.
TUTORIAL_MOVE: Move with the arrow keys, WASD, or right-click.
TUTORIAL_ATTACK: Attacking empty ground has no target. Click a monster, or face it and press Space to attack.
TUTORIAL_ATTACK_TARGET: You have selected a monster as your attack target. Stay in place and keep attacking until it is defeated.
TUTORIAL_ATTACK_HIT: Good. You attacked the monster. Defeating monsters grants experience and may drop items.
TUTORIAL_PICKUP: Left-click items dropped by monsters to pick them up.
TUTORIAL_HUNT_EXP: Defeat monsters to gain experience and level up.
TUTORIAL_LEVEL5: At level 5, you receive orbs that can improve your stats.
TUTORIAL_COMPLETE: Tutorial stage 2 is complete. You can now move to town.

Researching these new Magic Stones requires many samples; bring back Magic Stones that drop randomly when defeating Guardians and you will be rewarded.
INST_DUNGEON_1_DETAIL: The Guardians of the Matrix — the infinite space of the Ioten Temple — are said to operate using new Magic Stones unlike those of Iberium. Researching these new Magic Stones requires many samples; bring back Magic Stones that drop randomly when defeating Guardians and you will be rewarded.
INST_DUNGEON_2_DETAIL: The continuous expansion and upkeep of the Matrix — the infinite space of the Ioten Temple — is sustained by Magic Amplifiers that Ioten has installed throughout. To stop the expansion, all installed Magic Amplifiers must be destroyed. Destroy every Amplifier in the Matrix you enter to receive a reward.
INST_DUNGEON_3_DETAIL: The continuous expansion and upkeep of the Matrix — the infinite space of the Ioten Temple — is sustained by Magic Amplifiers that Ioten has installed throughout. To stop the expansion, all installed Magic Amplifiers must be destroyed. Destroy every Amplifier in the Matrix you enter to receive a reward.
INST_DUNGEON_4_DETAIL: Some of the Watchers — Guardians of the Matrix in the Ioten Temple — have entered a Berserk state for reasons unknown. Their increased attack power is disrupting our operations. To restore smooth operations, defeat the Berserk Watchers and you will be rewarded.
INST_DUNGEON_5_DETAIL: The Guardians of the Matrix — the infinite space of the Ioten Temple — are reportedly controlled by a being known as the Commander. Defeating the Commander should make dealing with the Guardians far easier. Please defeat the Commander to advance through the Matrix. Note: the Commander only appears after you find and defeat 5 Guardians known as Intimus.
INST_DUNGEON_6_DETAIL: The Guardians of the Matrix — the infinite space of the Ioten Temple — are said to be infinitely produced by a being known as Big Mama. Defeating Big Mama would halt their production and greatly aid Matrix exploration. Please defeat Big Mama, who is much like a queen bee. Note: upon entering you are given 6 numbers; you must destroy the Magic Amplifiers in the matching Matrix sections to open the path to Big Mama.

TUTORIAL_TD_01_01_1_BODY: Welcome to godius! Let's start by choosing the name you'll carry through this world.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_01_2_BODY:
Your nickname cannot be changed once set. Please choose carefully.
(Exclusion of symbols, numbers, special characters / Length limit)
TUTORIAL_TD_01_02_1_BODY: Great name! Now let's shape your character's appearance. Choose your gender first, then select your hairstyle from 20 options and your hair color from 9 shades.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_02_2_BODY: Please select in order: Gender → Hairstyle → Hair Color.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_03_1_BODY: Now for one of the most important decisions. Please choose both your main class and your craft. These cannot be changed later, so please take your time.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_03_2_BODY:
Main Class — Defines how you fight in the world of Godius.
Warrior — A balanced combat expert, skilled in both offense and defense.
Gladiator — Master of the battlefield through sheer strength and endurance. 
Rogue — A shadow-walker who subdues enemies with swift, precise strikes.
Mage — A wielder of powerful magic who can turn the tide of any battle. 
Cleric — A divine force who supports allies and smites enemies with sacred power. 

Secondary Craft — Choose a skill to enrich your journey beyond combat.
Tailor — Craft armor and garments to protect your allies.
Blacksmith — Forge weapons of formidable strength.
Alchemist — Brew potions and craft special items for any situation.
Bard — Inspire allies with music, granting powerful buffs in battle.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_04_1_BODY: Now let's set your base stats. Your allocation here will define your combat style, so please refer to your class's strengths before deciding.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_04_2_BODY:
Warriors and Gladiators are recommended to bring STR and VIT close to 15. Mages and Clerics will benefit most from high VIT, MEN, and INT.

Your starting SP is set to STR×2 upon character creation. Core resource for Warrior/Gladiator skills.
Your starting HP is set to VIT×2 upon character creation.
Your starting MP is set to MEN×2 upon character creation. Core resource for Mage/Cleric spells.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_05_1_BODY: We're almost there! godius is divided between two nations — Taferi and Velk. Please choose where you'd like to begin your journey.
TUTORIAL_TD_01_05_2_BODY:
Taferi — Begin your journey in the capital city of Militard.
Velk — Begin your journey in the capital city of Pagohol.

You are free to explore all of godius regardless of your choice. However, please be aware that players from opposing nations may be hostile toward you.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_01_1_BODY: Before your adventure begins, let's go over the basic controls.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_01_2_BODY:
Movement - Use the arrow keys, WASD, or right-click with your mouse to move your character.
Chat - Press Enter to activate the chat window at the bottom of the main screen. Type your message and press Enter again to send.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_02_1_BODY: You will not be able to leave the city until your character reaches level 6. Head to the Training Ground first to learn the basics!
TUTORIAL_TD_02_03_1_BODY: You've entered the Training Ground! Let's start with an introduction to Guards and Guard Zones.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_03_2_BODY:
Guards
Guards are NPCs that protect characters within a designated area. They are color-coded based on the type of zone they patrol.

Green Guard — Stationed in town areas.
Red Guard — Stationed in PK (Player Kill) zones.
Blue Guard — Stationed in Non-PK zones.

Guard Zone
A safe area located at the entrance of each map. Inside a Guard Zone, you are protected from attacks by monsters and other player characters.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_04_1_BODY: Next, let's go over the inventory. Managing your items well is key to a successful adventure.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_04_2_BODY:
Opening the Inventory - Press the ' (apostrophe) key or click the bag icon on screen to open your inventory.
Equipping Items - Right-click an item to equip it, or drag it to the appropriate equipment slot and left-click to confirm.
Checking Your Currency - Hover your mouse over the coin icon in the bottom-right of the inventory to view your current Lance (currency) balance.
Equipment Weight - Each piece of equipment has a weight value. Your carry limit is determined by your STR stat, so keep that in mind when equipping gear.
Durability - Equipment has durability. When it drops to 10% or below, a warning indicator will appear in the bottom-right of the main UI. If durability reaches 0, the item's effects are lost and it becomes unusable. Visit a Blacksmith and use the appropriate oil item to repair your gear before that happens.
Items with 0 durability can be restored through a separate recovery system.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_05_1_BODY: Finally, let's cover the basics of combat.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_05_2_BODY:
Press the Space Bar or use Left Mouse Click to perform a basic attack.
Now it's time to try it yourself! Once this guide is complete, you'll be free to begin hunting in the Training Ground.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_06_1_BODY: You've defeated your first monster! Experience points have been earned. Here's how to track your level and EXP.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_06_2_BODY:
Checking Level & EXP - Press the ] key to view your current level and experience points. Your EXP, HP, MP, and SP are also displayed in real time at the top-right of the main screen.
Experience Bar - Hover your mouse over the red EXP bar in the top-right of the main screen to see detailed values.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_07_1_BODY: You've found an item! Here's how to pick up and manage items in the field.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_07_2_BODY:
Picking Up Items - Left-click on any item dropped in the field to pick it up immediately.
Picking Up with Inventory Open - When your inventory is open, your mouse cursor changes to an item icon. In this state, clicking a field item will move it directly into your inventory.
Dropping Items - Click an item in your inventory, then left-click anywhere in the field to drop it.
Lance (LANS) - Lance is the basic currency of godius. Simply move your character over dropped Lance in the field to collect it automatically.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_08_1_BODY: You've reached level 5! It's time to grow stronger. Here's how to use the Orb of Power to develop your character.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_08_2_BODY:
Orb of Power - You receive Orbs of Power upon leveling up. These can be used to raise base parameters (STR, AGR, DEX, INT, VIT, MEN) or increase skill function levels.

Parameter Level          Orbs Required
1 ~ 15                     1 Orb
16 ~ 20                    3 Orbs
21 ~ 25                    4 Orbs
26 ~ 30                    5 Orbs

Stat Growth
WC, AC — Increased by raising parameters or equipping gear.
HC, DC — Increased by raising parameters, equipping gear, or leveling up.
HP, MP, SP — Increased by leveling up only. Growth rate varies depending on your main class.

Skill Function Level
Skill function levels can be raised up to a maximum of 30 using two methods.
Chance-based — Use 1 Orb of Power to attempt a +1 function level increase. Success is not guaranteed.
Guaranteed — Spend 2 to 15 Orbs of Power to raise function level with 100% success.

Success rates for the chance-based method decrease as function levels rise. Use both methods wisely to make the most of your Orbs of Power.
TUTORIAL_TD_02_09_1_BODY: You've reached level 6! You are now ready to venture beyond the city walls. Take what you've learned in the Training Ground and explore the world of Godius.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_01_1_BODY: Welcome back to the city! Before your adventure takes you further, let's go over some key features of Godius. First up — the map.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_01_2_BODY:
Opening the Map - Click the Map Board object near the city entrance, or press the Home key to open the map.

Map Types
City Map — Opens when accessed from within the city. Displays the full layout of the city.
World Map — Opens when accessed from the field. Displays the entire world of Gadius.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_02_1_BODY: The city is home to a variety of NPCs and objects. Here are the key facilities you'll be visiting often.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_02_2_BODY:
Trade Master - Click the Trade Master to enter the Item Exchange. Here you can buy and sell items with other players.
Mailbox - Click the mailbox object near the city square, or click the envelope icon in the top-left of the main UI to open the mail menu.
Notice Boards - Various notice boards are placed throughout the city — including Taferi/Velk boards, a Free Board, and an Announcement Board. Click any board object to open its menu.
Garrison Keeper - The Garrison Keeper can take you to the Union Garrison, where free PvP combat with other players is available. Inside the garrison, speak with the Champion NPC to participate in Arena matches.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_03_1_BODY: Here's how to manage your items and currency safely throughout your journey.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_03_2_BODY:
Inventory - Consumable items can be used immediately by right-clicking them.
Register frequently used items to quick slots 1–9 for fast access using the number keys or right-click.
With the inventory open, click the bag icon again to switch it to a trash icon. In this mode, left-click to delete a single consumable, or use Ctrl + left-click to delete all items of the same type at once.
Equipment items cannot be deleted.
Storage - Enter the Storage building in the city and click the Storage Box object to open your personal storage. Most items can be stored here, with the exception of certain special items. Storage can be expanded up to 3 slots (1 slot = 28×14 spaces).
Locker - A compact storage option separate from the main storage. Each locker holds up to 99 items of the same type, limited to consumables of 2×2 size or smaller, and is managed through the storage menu. Up to 7 lockers can be activated, but the number of keys required increases as more lockers are unlocked (up to 5 keys).
Bank - Visit the Bank building in the city and speak with the Bank Manager NPC to deposit or withdraw Lance (LANS).
TUTORIAL_TD_03_04_1_BODY: Here's how to communicate with other players in Gadius.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_04_2_BODY:
Opening the Chat - Press the Enter key to switch the main UI to chat mode. You can also open the chat by clicking any of the 5 option buttons above the chat window.
Chat Channels - Select your desired channel using the buttons at the top of the chat window. (Left to right)
General — Send messages to nearby players.
Guild — Send messages to guild members only.
Whisper / Trade — Send a private message to a specific player or request an item trade.
Party — Send messages to party members only.
Union — Send messages to members of your union.
TUTORIAL_TD_03_05_1_BODY: You've learned the essentials of Gadius! You are now ready to venture beyond the city and begin hunting in the field. The world of Gadius awaits you!
TUTORIAL_TD_04_01_1_BODY: Before you begin hunting, here's what you need to know about the hunting grounds.
TUTORIAL_TD_04_01_2_BODY:
PK Zone / Non-PK Zone
Non-PK Zone — Player versus player combat is completely restricted. Hunt safely without worry.
PK Zone — Players can attack and be attacked by other players. Proceed with caution.

Recommended Starter Hunting Grounds
Taferi — Lucrecia Swampland, Lucrecia Underground Path 1–3
Velk — Lampas Swampland, Lampas Underground Path 1–3

Map Types
Field Map — Open terrain that is easy to navigate.
Cave Map — Narrow corridor-style layouts that can be difficult to move through.

Each monster has resistances to one of four elements — Fire, Ice, Holy, or Neutral. Choose a hunting ground that suits your character's elemental affinity.
TUTORIAL_TD_04_02_1_BODY: You've made it to the field! Here's what you need to know about status effects and debuffs you may encounter in the field.
TUTORIAL_TD_04_02_2_BODY:
Status Effects - Active status effects are displayed as icons in the bottom-right of the main UI. (Left to right: Disease → Poison → Bind → Hold)
Petrification — If your character blocks a narrow passage such as a building entrance for a certain period of time, they will turn gray and be forcibly moved to the cemetery. Certain monsters can also inflict petrification. The effect is lifted after 30 minutes of staying logged in, or by using a Petrification Remedy on yourself or another character.
Poison — Causes continuous HP loss over time. Indicated by an icon in the bottom-right of the UI.
Disease — Negatively affects character stats and abilities. Indicated by an icon in the bottom-right of the UI.

Debuffs
Bind — Inflicted by an opponent's magic skill. Prevents movement for a set duration (approximately 1 minute). Automatically lifted after time expires, or can be removed immediately with the Release skill.
Hold — Inflicted by an opponent's magic skill. Prevents attacking and casting magic for a set duration (approximately 1 minute). Automatically lifted after time expires, or can be removed immediately with the Release skill.
TUTORIAL_TD_04_03_1_BODY: You're stronger together! Here's how to form a party with other players.
TUTORIAL_TD_04_03_2_BODY:
Sending a Party Request - Approach another character within 1 tile and press Ctrl + Right Mouse Click to send a party request. Once accepted, the party is formed and the player who initiated the request becomes the party leader. Party formation with characters from opposing nations is not available.
Party Window - Once a party is formed, the party window will open in the upper-right area of the screen, allowing you to monitor your party members' status in real time.
Party Experience - When hunting in a party, the experience gained from defeating monsters is doubled and distributed among all party members. Characters with higher levels will receive a greater share of the experience.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_01_1_BODY: Welcome back to the city! Let's go over the shops and trading options available to you.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_01_2_BODY:
General Shops - Each type of item can only be purchased at its designated specialty shop. However, you can sell items you own at any shop.

Special Facilities
Blacksmith — Repair and enhance your equipment here.
Inn — Change your appearance or browse costume options.
Tavern — Exchange coins, contribution points, and dungeon entry tickets.

1:1 Trade - Approach another character and press Ctrl + Left Mouse Click to send a direct trade request.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_02_1_BODY: Enhancing your equipment is one of the best ways to grow stronger. Here's how it works.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_02_2_BODY:
How to Enhance - Visit the Blacksmith NPC or the Blacksmith Guild Keeper at the Blacksmith to enhance your weapons and armor. Enhancement requires both currency and materials.
Enhancement Levels - Equipment can be enhanced up to a maximum of 9 levels. However, enhancements at higher levels carry a risk of failure. A failed enhancement may result in a level decrease or the permanent destruction of the item. Please proceed with caution.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_03_1_BODY: Learning skills will make your combat experience far more dynamic. Here's how to acquire and use them.
TUTORIAL_TD_05_03_2_BODY:
Learning Skills - Enter the Guild building corresponding to your main or secondary class and speak with the Guild Keeper NPC to learn available skills.
Using Magic Skills - Once learned, magic skills can be moved from the skill menu (shortcut key Z) to the quick slots in the top-left of the main UI. Use the F5–F9 keys for quick access during combat.
Warrior / Gladiator Special Skills & Common Skills - Most of these skills are automatically acquired as your function level rises. Each skill has a designated keyboard shortcut — please refer to the class-specific skill help guide for details.
